flashy.services package

Submodules

flashy.services.acquisition_service module

class flashy.services.acquisition_service.AcquisitionService(processing_config)

Bases: QObject

Central service responsible for managing acquisition and analysis pipelines.

This class orchestrates: - Acquisition worker threads (digitizer-specific) - Analysis worker threads (parallel processing) - Queue-based communication between acquisition and analysis stages

Each instance spawns multiple AnalysisWorker threads that process incoming data asynchronously.

The begin_acquisition method starts a new acquisition session using the selected digitizer backend.

Inherits:

PySide6.QtCore.QObject

acquisition_started

Emitted when acquisition starts.

acquisition_finished

Emitted when acquisition completes successfully.

acquisition_error

Emitted when an error occurs during acquisition.

stop_requested

Emitted when acquisition stop is requested.

processing_config: ProcessingConfig

Deep-copied processing configuration used internally.

acquisition_results: List[BatchPulses]

Accumulated analysis results per channel.

analysis_todo_queue

Queue of raw acquisition batches waiting for analysis.

discard_todo

Number of discarded batches due to full analysis queue.

printed_todo_queue

Flag preventing repeated queue-full warnings.

discard_comp

Number of discarded completed analysis results.

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("AcquisitionService" inherits "QObject": Methods:   #4 type=Signal, signature=acquisition_started()   #5 type=Signal, signature=acquisition_finished()   #6 type=Signal, signature=acquisition_error(QString), parameters=QString   #7 type=Signal, signature=stop_requested()   #8 type=Slot, signature=acquisition_event_dump(QVariantList), parameters=QVariantList   #9 type=Slot, signature=analysis_complete(PyObject), parameters=PyObject   #10 type=Slot, signature=stop_acquisition()   #11 type=Slot, signature=_on_worker_finished() )
printed_comp_queue

Flag preventing repeated completion warnings.

get_basic_dig_info(digitizer)

Retrieve basic information from the selected digitizer.

This method stops any currently running acquisition worker before querying the hardware.

Parameters:

digitizer (Literal["caen_dt5781"]) – Digitizer backend identifier.

Returns:

Dictionary containing digitizer metadata.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Raises:

ValueError – If the digitizer information cannot be retrieved.

begin_acquisition(digitizer)

Start a new acquisition session in a background worker thread.

Any currently running acquisition is stopped before starting a new one.

Parameters:

digitizer (Literal["caen_dt5781"]) – Digitizer backend to use.

acquisition_event_dump(batch)

Receive raw acquisition batches and enqueue them for analysis.

Parameters:

batch (list) – List of raw pulses.

analysis_complete(analysis_result)

Handle completed analysis results and store them in memory.

Parameters:

analysis_result (AnalysisResult) – Processed analysis output.

stop_acquisition()

Request the current acquisition worker to stop.

shutdown()

Gracefully shutdown all acquisition and analysis workers.

This should be called when the application exits or acquisition ends.

flashy.services.analysis_service module

class flashy.services.analysis_service.AnalysisService

Bases: object

Service to analyse pulses in different formats.

analyse_all_tdms_file(filename, analysis_config=None)

Analyse data contained in a TDMS file.

Parameters:

  • filename (str) – Path to the TDMS file.

  • analysis_config (AnalysisConfig | None, optional) – Analysis configuration to use. If None, a default configuration is used.

Returns:

Results of the analysis of the file’s pulses.

Return type:

AnalysisResult

analyse_real_time(batch, processing_config)

Perform real-time pulse analysis (used by AnalysisWorker).

Parameters:

  • batch (list[np.ndarray]) – Unordered list of pulses to analyse.

  • processing_config (ProcessingConfig) – Current processing configuration.

Returns:

Results of the analysis of the pulses.

Return type:

AnalysisResult

legacy_analyse_csv_file(path, analysis_config=None)

Analyse data contained in a CSV file using the default ProcessingConfig.

Warning

This method is legacy and only works for the Bergoz BCT with CAEN DT5781, as it was exclusively used by FLASHy 1.0.

Parameters:

  • path (str) – Path to the CSV file.

  • analysis_config (AnalysisConfig | None, optional) – Analysis configuration to use. If None, a default configuration is used.

Returns:

Analysis results of the file’s pulses, or None if analysis fails.

Return type:

AnalysisResult | None

flashy.services.analysis_worker module

class flashy.services.analysis_worker.AnalysisWorker(analysis_queue, config)

Bases: QThread

Worker thread that executes real-time analysis in a background loop.

This class wraps AnalysisService and continuously consumes data from a queue, processing it asynchronously and emitting results back to the main thread.

Inherits:

PySide6.QtCore.QThread

analysis_complete

Emitted when a batch has been successfully analysed.

queue: queue.Queue[list]

Queue of incoming data batches to be processed.

config: ProcessingConfig

Processing configuration used for analysis.

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("AnalysisWorker" inherits "QThread": Methods:   #12 type=Signal, signature=analysis_complete(PyObject), parameters=PyObject )
analyser: AnalysisService

Service responsible for performing analysis computations.

run()

Main thread loop.

Continuously retrieves batches from the queue and processes them using AnalysisService. Results are emitted via analysis_complete.

The loop blocks briefly on the queue (timeout = 0.1s) to allow responsive shutdown.

stop()

Request the worker thread to stop execution.

The thread will exit after the next queue timeout.

flashy.services.data_loader module

class flashy.services.data_loader.DataLoader

Bases: object

Service for reading data files and returning parsed content.

The methods of this class return an AcquisitionConfig and an AnalysisConfig (except legacy CSV reading), with data separated per channel. This allows the AnalysisService to construct a ProcessingConfig from these components or use a user-provided analysis configuration.

read_all_tdms_file(filename)

Read a TDMS file and return its contents.

Parameters:

filename (str) – Path to the TDMS file.

Returns:

A tuple containing: - Acquisition configuration used when saving - Analysis configuration used when saving - Dictionary of channel data

Return type:

tuple[AcquisitionConfig, AnalysisConfig, dict[str, List[dict[str, Any]]]]

read_config_json_file()

Read the configuration file generated when the program was previously closed (config.json).

Returns:

Previously saved user and processing configuration.

Return type:

tuple[UserConfig, ProcessingConfig]

legacy_read_csv_file(path)

Read a legacy CSV file from past shoots using the default FLASHy 1.0 configuration.

Parameters:

path (str) – Path to the CSV file.

Returns:

A tuple containing: - Acquisition configuration (default FLASHy 1.0) - Analysis configuration (default FLASHy 1.0) - Dictionary containing pulse data per channel

Return type:

tuple[AcquisitionConfig, AnalysisConfig, dict[str, List[dict[str, Any]]]] | None

flashy.services.export_service module

class flashy.services.export_service.DataExporter

Bases: object

Service to export data to disk.

write_post_acquisition_to_tdms(data, file_path, processing_config, acquisition_type='run')

Write acquired data to a TDMS file.

Parameters:

  • data (List[BatchPulses]) – List of BatchPulses, one per digitizer channel.

  • file_path (str) – Destination file path.

  • processing_config (ProcessingConfig) – Processing configuration used during acquisition.

  • acquisition_type (str) – Context describing why the file was saved.

save_config_to_json(user_config, processing_config)

Save configuration to a JSON file when closing the program.

Parameters:

  • user_config (UserConfig) – Current user configuration.

  • processing_config (ProcessingConfig) – Current processing configuration.

write_all_rawdata_to_tdms(data, file_path, processing_config, acquisition_type='run')

Legacy method for writing raw pulse data to a TDMS file.

Warning

This method is not used anymore. It has been replaced by write_post_acquisition_to_tdms with a new data format.

Parameters:

  • data (List[List[Any]]) – Raw pulse data to write to disk.

  • file_path (str) – Destination file path.

  • processing_config (ProcessingConfig) – Processing configuration used during acquisition.

  • acquisition_type (str) – Context describing why the file was saved.

flashy.services.pulse_processor module

class flashy.services.pulse_processor.PulseProcessor

Bases: object

Service responsible for processing batches of pulses. This service exposes the process_pulses method used primarily by AnalysisService.

process_pulses(batch)

Process a BatchPulses instance according to its configuration.

If no valid pulse is detected by _clean_data, the original batch is returned unchanged.

Processing pipeline:

  • Select valid pulses (ALL)

  • Send valid pulses to disk (ALL)

  • Level data (analysis-specific)

  • Convert LSB → V (digitizer-specific: gain & ADC resolution)

  • Compute area (analysis + digitizer sampling period)

  • Convert V·µs → nC (detector-specific calibration factor)

  • Convert nC → cGy (analysis-specific calibration factor)

Parameters:

batch (BatchPulses) – Batch of pulses to process.

Returns:

Processed batch containing analysed pulse results (or original batch if no valid pulses).

Return type:

BatchPulses
